title: Feed Discovery, WHATWG URL Identity, Redirects, and SSRF · Mastery Check
summary: A release reviewer compares near-miss TypeScript decisions for feed discovery, whatwg url identity, redirects, and ssrf and accepts only runtime and compiler evidence, explicit non-claims, and named browser, Node, network, XML, PostgreSQL, handle, load, accessibility, deployment, and production transfer gates. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for authorized TypeScript discovery policy. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, c…
objectives: Distinguish direct feeds from bounded HTML autodiscovery and require explicit source intent and supported media evidence.
Admit supported alternate RSS or Atom links, resolve against the final document URL, deduplicate, rank transparently, and bound candidates.
Use WHATWG URL parsing and a documented equivalence policy without collapsing query, case, port, path, fragment, or percent-encoding distinctions unsafely.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Feed aggregation and TypeScript Diagnose the hidden failure · Quiz Feed Ts Discovery Audit
steps.instruction:
quiz case — A quiz TypeScript team handles case 6202 during an inclusive cross-device research reader review. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to reconcile direct and autodiscovery, URL identity, redirects, SSRF, and provenance; failed, canceled, stale, and cross-tenant work must preserve the prior admitted state; then change one emitted artifact, package, container, incident, backup, restore, rollback, or capacity condition and reconcile evidence. Browser TypeScript uses pure bounded models and original RSS, Atom, HTTP, PostgreSQL, and reader fixtures only. Real DNS, Fetch, streams, fast-xml-parser, Express, PostgreSQL, processes, signals, handle and load claims, containers, accessibility review, backups, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Retain submitted, parsed, resolved, redirected, selected, and rejected URL evidence with tenant, actor, policy, and bounded causes.
